name: ◁ Classic Walnut Baklawa
servings: prep time: 30 minutes, plus 1 hour cooling

ingredients.item:
SIMPLE SYRUP
2½ cups (500 g) granulated sugar
Squeeze of fresh lemon juice
ingredients.original:
SIMPLE SYRUP
2½ cups (500 g) granulated sugar
Squeeze of fresh lemon juice

ingredients.item:
WALNUT FILLING
1½ pounds (700 g) unsalted whole raw walnuts (see Notes)
3 tablespoons ground cardamom
ingredients.original:
WALNUT FILLING
1½ pounds (700 g) unsalted whole raw walnuts (see Notes)
3 tablespoons ground cardamom

ingredients.item:
PHYLLO PASTRY
½ cup (120 g) unsalted butter, melted
½ cup (120 g) olive oil
ingredients.original:
PHYLLO PASTRY
½ cup (120 g) unsalted butter, melted
½ cup (120 g) olive oil

ingredients.item: 1 box (16 ounces, or 454 g) frozen phyllo dough (2 rolls; 18 sheets total), thawed for 1 hour
ingredients.original: 1 box (16 ounces, or 454 g) frozen phyllo dough (2 rolls; 18 sheets total), thawed for 1 hour

ingredients.item: 2 TO MAKE THE SIMPLE SYRUP: In a medium saucepan, combine the sugar and lemon juice with 1¼ cups (300 ml) of water and bring to a boil over medium heat. Once boiling, reduce the heat to medium-low and let simmer for 10 minutes; it should have a runny, syrupy consistency. Remove the pan from the heat and let cool.
ingredients.original: 2 TO MAKE THE SIMPLE SYRUP: In a medium saucepan, combine the sugar and lemon juice with 1¼ cups (300 ml) of water and bring to a boil over medium heat. Once boiling, reduce the heat to medium-low and let simmer for 10 minutes; it should have a runny, syrupy consistency. Remove the pan from the heat and let cool.

ingredients.item: 3 MEANWHILE, MAKE THE WALNUT FILLING: Add the walnuts to a food processor and grind until a coarse consistency; each walnut should break up to about a fifth of its size.
ingredients.original: 3 MEANWHILE, MAKE THE WALNUT FILLING: Add the walnuts to a food processor and grind until a coarse consistency; each walnut should break up to about a fifth of its size.

ingredients.item: 4 Transfer the ground walnuts to a medium bowl and add the cardamom, sugar, and egg. Mix well using a spoon.
ingredients.original: 4 Transfer the ground walnuts to a medium bowl and add the cardamom, sugar, and egg. Mix well using a spoon.

ingredients.item: 5 TO PREPARE THE PHYLLO PASTRY: In a small bowl, combine the butter and oil.
ingredients.original: 5 TO PREPARE THE PHYLLO PASTRY: In a small bowl, combine the butter and oil.

ingredients.item: 6 Unwrap the phyllo dough package, unroll it, and cover the dough with a kitchen towel to prevent it from drying out while you work. Measure the sheets’ dimensions against your pan (I used a 13 x 18-inch, or 33 x 46-cm, rimmed sheet pan) and snip any excess using scissors; the phyllo sheets should sit inside the pan up to the edges.
ingredients.original: 6 Unwrap the phyllo dough package, unroll it, and cover the dough with a kitchen towel to prevent it from drying out while you work. Measure the sheets’ dimensions against your pan (I used a 13 x 18-inch, or 33 x 46-cm, rimmed sheet pan) and snip any excess using scissors; the phyllo sheets should sit inside the pan up to the edges.

ingredients.item: 7 To assemble, generously brush the bottom of the pan with the butter-oil mixture, then layer 2 sheets of phyllo on top, ensuring you smooth them down and remove air bubbles as best you can every time you add a sheet. Brush the top sheet of phyllo generously with the butter-oil mixture, then continue layering 2 sheets at a time, brushing the top sheet with the butter-oil mixture, until you have 8 sheets (or half the phyllo dough) layered.
ingredients.original: 7 To assemble, generously brush the bottom of the pan with the butter-oil mixture, then layer 2 sheets of phyllo on top, ensuring you smooth them down and remove air bubbles as best you can every time you add a sheet. Brush the top sheet of phyllo generously with the butter-oil mixture, then continue layering 2 sheets at a time, brushing the top sheet with the butter-oil mixture, until you have 8 sheets (or half the phyllo dough) layered.

ingredients.item: 8 Spoon the walnut filling onto the phyllo pastry, covering it evenly and packing it down using the back of a spoon or your hand. Layer the remaining 8 phyllo sheets, brushing them with the butter-oil mixture, as you did in step 7.
ingredients.original: 8 Spoon the walnut filling onto the phyllo pastry, covering it evenly and packing it down using the back of a spoon or your hand. Layer the remaining 8 phyllo sheets, brushing them with the butter-oil mixture, as you did in step 7.

ingredients.item: 9 Using a sharp knife, gently make straight cuts parallel to the long edge of the pan, roughly 1½ inches (4 cm) apart, all the way through to the bottom. Hold down the pastry gently as you cut and use a rocking motion so that the pastry does not get dragged out of place.
ingredients.original: 9 Using a sharp knife, gently make straight cuts parallel to the long edge of the pan, roughly 1½ inches (4 cm) apart, all the way through to the bottom. Hold down the pastry gently as you cut and use a rocking motion so that the pastry does not get dragged out of place.

ingredients.item: 10 Make a diagonal cut from one corner of the pan, going all the way to the opposite corner, then make more diagonal cuts parallel to that, about 1 inch (2.5 cm) apart, to create diamond-shape pieces. Go over all the cuts twice to ensure you have cut through all the layers. (If it is easier, you can simply cut them into square shapes and adjust the size to your liking.)
ingredients.original: 10 Make a diagonal cut from one corner of the pan, going all the way to the opposite corner, then make more diagonal cuts parallel to that, about 1 inch (2.5 cm) apart, to create diamond-shape pieces. Go over all the cuts twice to ensure you have cut through all the layers. (If it is easier, you can simply cut them into square shapes and adjust the size to your liking.)

ingredients.item: 11 Bake for 45 to 50 minutes, until the baklawa is a light golden color. Remove from the oven and immediately pour the cooled simple syrup over the top; you will hear a splashing sound as the cooled syrup hits the hot baklawa. Garnish with the crushed pistachios and rose petals (if using).
ingredients.original: 11 Bake for 45 to 50 minutes, until the baklawa is a light golden color. Remove from the oven and immediately pour the cooled simple syrup over the top; you will hear a splashing sound as the cooled syrup hits the hot baklawa. Garnish with the crushed pistachios and rose petals (if using).

ingredients.item: 12 Let the baklawa cool for at least 1 hour in the pan before serving. If you plate it while hot, the baklawa will crumble.
ingredients.original: 12 Let the baklawa cool for at least 1 hour in the pan before serving. If you plate it while hot, the baklawa will crumble.
